evidence.snippet:
Drone Recovery Systems
Rocketman Parachutes is proud to manufacture **the lightest, strongest, and most compact drone recovery systems on the market**. Many of our systems are fully **plug-and-play**, tailored to your aircraft’s mounting geometry, deployment method, and available internal packing volume.

items.notes: Parachutes, deployment bags, shock cords, pilot chutes, and harness systems designed for drones/UAVs as well as rockets and high-altitude balloons. Primary UXV application is drone/UAV parachute recovery.

evidence.snippet:
**Address:**
Rocketman Parachutes
8337 Penn Ave South
Bloomington, MN 55431
Some of the products that we offer are Ultra-Light High-Performance Parachutes, Spring Loaded Pilot Chutes, and Packs. We can also custom make harnesses and ultra-light soft links.

For custom projects, simply provide the **payload weight**, **packing volume**, and **desired deployment configuration**, and we will engineer a full recovery system to meet mission-specific requirements.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Manufactures parachute recovery components (canopies, packs, deployment bags, shock cords, pilot chutes, harnesses) sold as parts integrated into drone/UAV systems.

products.notes: Vendor also sells broader rocket parachute lines (rocketry, HAB) and deployment hardware (The TD-2, The Piranha Kit, The Breach) and resells Jolly Logic altimeters. UXV-specific product lines are listed here.

ndaa.notes: Site prominently claims 'Made in the USA' and uses US-sourced materials, but makes no explicit NDAA Section 848/889 compliance claim.

evidence.snippet: ### _**Industry - Leading Parachutes designed for Performance and Reliability. Made in the USA, flown around the world, and trusted since 1969.

readiness.notes: Long-established vendor (since 1969) with catalog products at listed prices across multiple drone sizes. Bargain Bin page references extras from large production runs.

tagline: Manufactures parachutes and recovery components for model rockets, high-altitude balloons, and unmanned systems, including ultra-light drone parachutes, deployment bags, and shock cords. Primary market appears to be hobbyist rocketry; public evidence of defense/federal contracting is absent. NDAA compliance status and Blue UAS certification unknown. No visible tie to named prime contracts or programs.
